title: Client Storage Schema Versioning & Migration
summary: How established libraries and the web platform evolve PERSISTED client state safely when the schema that wrote it changes. Surveys the native IndexedDB version/onupgradeneeded model, redux-persist (version + createMigrate + _persist envelope), Zustand persist (version + migrate, discard-on-miss), TanStack Query persistClient (buster string = discard-only, no migration), localForage (mechanism-only, no versioning), and the idb wrapper. Two orthogonal axes fall out: DETECTION (an explicit version stamp vs. structural re-validation à la Zod safeParse) and MISMATCH POLICY (migrate-forward through an ordered registry vs. discard→defaults). The dominant ergonomic pattern is an envelope + ordered migration registry run lazily on read, with discard→defaults as the safe fallback when no migration path exists — and the native platform (IndexedDB) shows versioning is intrinsically a facet of the persistence layer, applied per store/scope at read/open time, not a separate product. Grounds decision #1251 (placement + shape of a webstates schema-versioning facet) — why webstates (storage domain) over webreliability (whose ratified scope is operation-failure recovery, not data-shape evolution), why a thin engine-agnostic layer above CustomStorageStrategy over baking it per-strategy, and why discard→defaults is the most-resilient default.
